content: The autonomous curiosity drive from curiosity-driven reinforcement learning agents can be framed as a built-in active learning signal for pruning and updating the 2024-2025 SOTA agent working memory, creating a self-sustaining memory system that only retains information the agent inherently finds meaningful for future exploration. This eliminates the need for external human labeling to curate agent memory, aligning memory retention directly with the agent's ongoing exploration goals.
